audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in os-local.c are safe: `srv->lud_host` (path) is explicitly null-checked at line 319 before use; `srv` itself is guaranteed non-null by the caller chain (request.c:488 guards `*srvp != NULL`, and open.c:439 dereferences `srv` before reaching os-local.c); `ld` is guaranteed non-null by LDAP handle lifecycle; `sa` in `ldap_pvt_connect` is always `&server` (stack variable). The `CMSG_FIRSTHDR` result in the `LDAP_PF_LOCAL_SENDMSG` block is non-null because the control buffer is exactly `CMSG_SPACE(sizeof(int))` bytes.
